examples.title: Algorithm: Exact String Comparison
examples.description: Compares two strings for exact equality
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CompareStrings
INPUT: str1, str2
OUTPUT: boolean result

BEGIN
 IF length(str1) != length(str2) THEN
 result = FALSE
 ELSE
 FOR i = 0 TO length(str1) - 1 DO
 IF str1[i] != str2[i] THEN
 result = FALSE
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 IF i == length(str1) THEN
 result = TRUE
 END IF
 END IF
 RETURN result
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode compares two strings character by character. If the lengths are different, it immediately returns FALSE. Otherwise, it iterates through each character and returns FALSE if a mismatch is found. If the loop completes without finding a mismatch, it returns TRUE.

examples.title: Java: Exact String Comparison
examples.description: Compares two strings for exact equality using Java's String.equals() method
examples.explanation: This Java code uses the built-in String.equals() method to compare two strings for exact equality.

examples.title: Java: Lexicographic String Comparison
examples.description: Compares two strings lexicographically using Java's String.compareTo() method
examples.explanation: This Java code uses the built-in String.compareTo() method to compare two strings lexicographically.
